meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control for Freelancers: A Checklist
content.seo_description: Stop losing code and start billing right. A freelancer's guide to mastering SaaS pricing and version control using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Solve version control chaos by integrating automated infrastructure-as-code with your pricing logic. By coupling Antigravity's state management with Claude Code's generative CLI, you create a self-documenting repository. This ensures every pricing tier change is versioned, auditable, and ready for client delivery without manual overhead or deployment risks.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, sweating because your client’s pricing model just broke, and you have no idea which commit—or lack thereof—caused the regression. The 'cowboy coding' era of freelancing is a liability that costs you hours of billable time and your reputation. 

True professional velocity isn't about typing faster; it's about building a system that guards your work. By implementing strict version control alongside your SaaS pricing architecture, you transform from a reactive freelancer into an architect of scalable, resilient revenue engines.

steps.title: Initialize Version-Controlled Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish a base repository that tracks your pricing tiers as code, ensuring every change is immutable and traceable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular JSON schema for a multi-tiered SaaS pricing model including trial periods, monthly/yearly billing, and feature flagging support.

steps.title: Automate Pricing Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the validation logic for your pricing tiers, ensuring type safety before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript utility that validates pricing tier configurations against the master schema to prevent invalid billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate validation logic for pricing tiers in src/billing/schema.ts'

steps.title: Implement Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC)
steps.description: Define your billing infrastructure (Stripe/LemonSqueezy) as code to sync with your app versioning.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration file that maps my SaaS pricing tiers to payment gateway product IDs, ensuring parity between local dev and production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map pricing tiers to Stripe API product IDs'

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing for Billing
steps.description: Ensure no pricing change breaks existing client access by running automated tests on every push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that mocks Stripe webhooks to verify pricing tier upgrades and downgrades function correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add unit tests for billing webhooks'

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline Audit
steps.description: Finalize your CI/CD pipeline to reject any pricing deployment that lacks a corresponding version tag.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a GitHub Actions workflow that enforces semantic versioning and prevents deployment if the pricing schema has changed without a tag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure CI/CD to enforce versioning on pricing deployments'

pro_tips: Always store Stripe Product IDs in environment-specific config files, never hardcoded in your business logic.
Use Git hooks to automatically append the current Git hash to your database pricing logs for instant traceability.
Implement 'Shadow Pricing' in your DB to test new tiers against real data before exposing them to customers.
Treat your pricing documentation as code; if it isn't in the README, it doesn't exist for the client.
Set up a 'Kill Switch' flag in your pricing code that reverts to a basic tier via a single environment variable change.
content.next_steps: Audit your current project for hardcoded pricing values, initialize Git, and migrate your logic into the structured schema defined in this guide.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping with the confidence of a lead architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
